title: Measuring OD 600
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"erc7q","text":"Measuring the optical density (OD) of a bacterial culture, typically at 600 nm, determines cell density by measuring light scattering using a spectrophotometer. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Click setup and set the wavelength to 600 nm.
Open the computer connected to the Carry UV60

Open the "Simple Reads" Desktop application. When you first open the software, the measure will do some manual calibrations. The power button will blink orange. Ensure the sample holder cover is shut completely.
Insert your "Blank". The best blank for an OD600 is fresh LB. When inserting any sample, always wipe the outside with a Kimwipe
Press "Zero" then "Read" The adsobance should now read ~0. Ignore if it reads a negative number.

Now insert your sample(s) and record the OD. If the OD value exceeds 1.0, the measurement becomes inaccurate due to multiple scattering. You can dilute the sample with fresh medium, record the dilution factor, measure the diluted sample, and multiply the reading by the dilution factor (e.g., if you dilute 1:10 and read 0.5, the actual OD is 5.0).
Ensure the sample holder cover is shut completely.
Under a flame, obtain 1 mL of each sample and place in a disposable 1.5 mL Cuvette Ensure the cuvettes are labeled at the top with the glycerol stock number
Under a flame, obtain 1 mL of the fresh LB and place in a disposable 1.5 mL Cuvette Never touch the cuvette in the detection window. Only hold from the top.
All samples must be neutralized with 10% bleach for at least 30 min to 1hr. There should be a noticeable foaming and a slight color change.
creator: Tamra Mbeuh Lahom Lot
